task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 07 Function description of the behavior of the tissue wipe unit

section.p: The basic behavior of the tissue wipe unit can be divided in two sections:

li.p: To maintain the optimal condition of the printheads, regular cleaning is essential.
This cleaning process occurs at the beginning of each cold/warm startup of the printer and is repeated every 2 hours.
Corrective cleaning may be required in response to specific incidents or printhead conditions.
These cleaning actions are conditional and automatically performed when curtain conditions apply.
When a significant number of nozzles fail at the start of a print job, a corrective cleaning process is initiated.
However, if the White printhead is not utilized in that particular print job, its condition is disregarded.
Once there's a need to use the White printhead again, its condition will be taken into account.
Another reason for corrective cleaning is a major decrease in nozzle performance during printing.
If a media touch or group failure is detected during printing, corrective cleaning is scheduled to occur at the end of the print job

note.p: The exclusion of the White-printhead as part of the corrective cleaning will result in a temporary decrease in printhead score (See chapter Print Quality -> FLOG).
As the printhead will be recovered when it's used again this decrease has no effect on the print quality of the White-printhead.

section.title: Spitting
section.p: Spitting, the process of jetting ink, is performed to maintain the dryness of the nozzle plate and ensure the proper condition of the nozzles.
This action can take place during standby and printing.
Spitting within the purge tray unit occurs under the following conditions:

li.p: After every cold/warm startup of the engine.
